text:
In a thrilling competition in the Bercy Arena at the Paris Olympics, the Japanese men won the team title, narrowly edging archrival China, while team USA captured a long-awaited bronze medal.

With a three point lead, China was in a prime position to take the gold with one rotation to go. But on their last event, high bar where Japan delivered great performances producing three scores in the mid 14s while China faltered.Su Weidemissed his layout Tkatchev and then a Kolman, which was the turning point in the outcome for the two teams.

Japanese team membersHashimoto Daiki,Kaya Kazuma,Oka Shinnosuke,Sugino TakaakiandTanigawa Watarurecorded 259.594 for the win, whileLiu Yang,Su Weide,Xiao Ruoteng,Zhang BohengandZou Jingyuanof China totaled 259.062 for the silver medal.

“It’s very different from winning as an individual”, said defending all-around champion Hashimoto. “Seeing everyone smiling, hugging, and celebrating deepened our bond. It was a medal that drove us to new heights and brought out feelings I never had.”

Japan was second to Russia at the last Olympics in Tokyo, and last won the team title at the Rio 2016 Olympics. While China was third in Tokyo, and last earned the team Olympic gold in 2012. Defending champion Russia is not present at this Olympic Games.

“I don’t think we need to comfort each other. We are a team, and we bear all the mistakes or poor performances together. So now, we move on and focus on the individual apparatus”, world and Olympic ring’s champion Liu Yang of China said after the competition.

The U.S. team ofAsher Hong,Paul Juda,Brody Malone,Stephen NedoroscikandFrederick Richardearned the bronze with 257.793. The Americans were nearly flawless during the competition hitting 18-out-of-18 routines, to win their first team medal since 2008, when they also earned the bronze.

“We feel absolutely ecstatic. This has been nothing short of a group effort. Every single person had a role today and they were all just as important as the next. Every single routine mattered. And every single routine, I knew that every single person had the ability to do that routine”, Paul Juda said. “Today we demonstrated that we keep together as a squad and we’re proud to be Americans and representing the US.”

With only one major mistake, on vault, Great Britain finished fourth with 255.527, followed by Ukraine 254.761 who like the U.S. hit every single routine. Rounding out the top eight in the final were Italy 248.260, Switzerland 247.427 and Canada 245.426.

The competitions at the Paris Olympics continue tomorrow with the women’s team final.

Vanesa Mašová of Czechia teamed with U.S. gymnast David Shamah to capture the mixed-pairs Sokol Grand Prix, one of two competitions held in memory of legendary Czech gymnast Vera Caslavska this weekend in Brno, Czechia.

After notching perfect scores in two of their three performances, Olympic gold medalist Jordan Chiles and professional dance partner Ezra Sosa earned third place in ABC’s “Dancing with the Stars” season finale on Tuesday.

Double victor Camilo Vera of Colombia, Xiang Yina of China, Nishiyama Misa of Japan, Ojima Nao of Japan and AIN Arsenii Dukhno captured titles on the second of two days of apparatus finals at the Junior World Championships in Manila on Monday.

China and the U.S. each claimed two golds, and an AIN gymnast took the fifth title, on the first of two days of apparatus finals at the Junior World Championships in Manila on Sunday.
